name: Roy Hargrove
abstract: Roy Anthony Hargrove (October 16, 1969 – November 2, 2018) was an American jazz musician and composer whose principal instruments were the trumpet and flugelhorn. He achieved critical acclaim after winning two Grammy Awards for differing styles of jazz in 1998 and 2002.

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part II
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Dizzy Gillespie extensively ('I idolized him so much', 'I had such a ball playing with him'). Roy Hargrove is mentioned only in a list of other trumpet players ('Jon Faddis, Claudio Roditi. Roy Hargrove. Every night a different trumpet player.') with no personal interaction
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 18, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Clark Terry
about_quotes.evidence: he’s a very special kid to me because my wife Gwen knows his mother very well... they were basically responsible for his education. They’ve gotten funds from different sources, now even I kicked in a few bucks, and sent him to Berklee
about_quotes.doc_title: Clark Terry interviewed by Joe Williams and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, May 19, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ... whether it’s Roy Hargrove or Joshua Redman and others ... see how now the setting has changed. ... [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, you mean in other words they can get themselves a manager ...
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I was also checking out a lot of the newer music that was being recorded at the time, whether it was Kenny Garrett or Joshua Redman or Roy Hargrove. I just wanted to know what guys were doing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Palmer
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve gotten great trumpet players who never heard of Roy Hargrove you know? And he was on the scene for many decades up until less than ten years ago when he passed away.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Palmer interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 2, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: There’s a young trumpet player, Roy Hargrove, only about 23 years old, he just signed a big contract for more money than I’ve ever made all my life.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
